global_positive_contract: western dark gothic fantasy board-game art
layered fabric, parchment, and dry edge wear
Paisley as secondary contrast context only

cutout_default.strategy: Use BiRefNet with foreground ML and focused orange despill for premium unit art. Use chroma key only as a rejected/control comparison on complex units.

cases.prompt: Create a full-body centered Gamble Battle unit character in western dark gothic fantasy board-game art, about 10 percent grounded realism, premium tabletop-card painting, dry powder-matte skin, de-shined velvet cloth, dull aged metal, parchment, soot, ink, matte gouache, dry brush, high-detail matte gothic illustration, layered fabric, parchment, and dry edge wear, hand-painted surface breakup, Vellum-level dry detail richness, Paisley as secondary contrast context only, heavy occlusion shadows, grim low-sheen gothic realism, grounded adult proportions, rough dry material texture, low-specular ambient light, broad heavy shadow, clean readable game-board silhouette, flat solid safety-orange #f84401 background, no text, logo, watermark. Vellum is a gothic contract witch wrapped in dry black cloth, dull corset armor, parchment clauses, wax seals, and ink-tendril magic. Preserve the elegant occult silhouette, long dark dress, paper fragments, and contract-sorcery read. Surface rule: absolutely no sweaty skin, wet highlights, glossy leather, shiny latex, plastic skin, polished splash-art reflections, lacquered armor, reflective armor, bright specular highlights, polished bevels, smooth airbrushed armor, cartoon/comic rendering, clean fantasy splash-art polish, or bright rim-light shine. Detail-richness rule: de-shining must preserve tactile dry detail, layered costume/material storytelling, small gothic accents, dry scratches, dust, worn edges, and hand-painted texture; do not simplify the unit into low-detail smooth shapes. Any highlight must look like dry paint on real cloth, parchment, dust, bone, or dull metal. Board-scale rule: keep detail grouped into large readable shapes; avoid confetti detail, tangled micro-straps, tiny background particles, and noisy floating fragments.
cases.negative_prompt: sweaty, glossy, shiny, wet, oily, plastic, latex, lacquered, polished leather, reflective armor, bright specular highlights, polished bevels, smooth airbrushed armor, cartoon, comic-book, toy-like proportions, clean fantasy render, heroic mobile-game lighting, anime, gacha, chibi, cute mascot, neon cyberpunk, mobile-game splash art, low-detail smooth creature model, over-smoothed simplified matte shapes, palette-only match, hyper-detailed chaos, busy background, textured background, floor shadow, cropped body, text, logo, watermark
acceptance: Goth direction stays intact.
Face and hands are dry and matte, not sweaty.
Black outfit reads as velvet or cloth, not shiny leather.
Parchment and ink fragments survive the cutout review.

cases.prompt: Create a full-body centered Gamble Battle unit character in western dark gothic fantasy board-game art, about 10 percent grounded realism, premium tabletop-card painting, dry powder-matte skin, de-shined velvet cloth, dull aged metal, parchment, soot, ink, matte gouache, dry brush, high-detail matte gothic illustration, layered fabric, parchment, and dry edge wear, hand-painted surface breakup, Vellum-level dry detail richness, Paisley as secondary contrast context only, heavy occlusion shadows, grim low-sheen gothic realism, grounded adult proportions, rough dry material texture, low-specular ambient light, broad heavy shadow, clean readable game-board silhouette, flat solid safety-orange #f84401 background, no text, logo, watermark. Age the style up into classy gothic stage-magic: matte black cloth, dry ivory cuffs, muted stained-glass color accents, and dusty powder highlights. Bubble rule: make exactly two large smoky ink-orb bubbles, one physically touching or overlapping each raised palm, with no detached satellite bubbles, no tiny bubble particles, and no soap-bubble rainbow glare. Surface rule: absolutely no sweaty skin, wet highlights, glossy leather, shiny latex, plastic skin, polished splash-art reflections, lacquered armor, reflective armor, bright specular highlights, polished bevels, smooth airbrushed armor, cartoon/comic rendering, clean fantasy splash-art polish, or bright rim-light shine. Any highlight must look like dry paint on real cloth, parchment, dust, bone, dull metal, or smoky ink glass. Board-scale rule: keep the two hand-held bubbles and flared-pants silhouette readable; avoid confetti detail, tangled micro-straps, tiny sparkles, busy particle clouds, and noisy floating fragments.

cases.prompt: Create a centered Gamble Battle ability token in western dark gothic fantasy board-game art, about 10 percent grounded realism, premium tabletop-card painting, powder-matte parchment, de-shined velvet cloth, dull aged metal, parchment, soot, ink, matte gouache, dry brush, high-detail matte gothic illustration, layered fabric, parchment, and dry edge wear, hand-painted surface breakup, Vellum-level dry detail richness, Paisley as secondary contrast context only, heavy occlusion shadows, grim low-sheen gothic realism, rough dry material texture, low-specular ambient light, broad heavy shadow, clean readable game-board silhouette, flat solid safety-orange #f84401 background, no text, logo, watermark. Asset concept: Vellum contract mark, a dry parchment sigil folded around a chalky matte charcoal contract seal, dry ink claw scratches, and abstract occult notch marks that are decorative shapes only, readable as a small circular ability token. Surface rule: absolutely no wet highlights, glossy wax, shiny seal, glossy leather, shiny latex, plastic material, polished splash-art reflections, lacquered metal, reflective armor, bright specular highlights, polished bevels, smooth airbrushed armor, cartoon/comic rendering, clean fantasy splash-art polish, or bright rim-light shine. Detail-richness rule: de-shining must preserve tactile dry detail, layered parchment/material storytelling, small gothic accents, dry scratches, dust, worn edges, and hand-painted texture; do not simplify the asset into low-detail smooth shapes. The seal must look like dry charcoal-black clay or chalky dead wax with no specular highlight ring. Any highlight must look like dry paint on parchment, dust, matte wax, bone, or dull metal. Board-scale rule: keep the seal, parchment fold, and ink claw marks readable; avoid confetti detail, tiny sparkles, busy particle clouds, noisy floating fragments, readable letters, readable numbers, runic alphabets, and text-like glyph rows.
